title: AST Burglary, Terroristic Threatening, MIW, Felony Assault: Fairbanks [AK25075736]
incident_type: Burglary, Terroristic Threatening, MIW, Felony Assault
summary: On July 30, 2025, at approximately 1618 hours, Fairbanks AST responded to the area of 2016 College Road for a report of a shirtless male threatening several people with a firearm and unlawfully entering a dwelling.SWAT was deployed to assist in the arrest of Kenneth Reed Jr, Age 62, for Burglary, Terroristic Threatening, Misconduct involving weapons in the fourth degree, and multiple felony assaults. Kenneth was arrested and remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center without further incident. AST thanks UAFPD and FPD for their assistance.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Burglary, Terroristic Threatening, MIW, Felony Assault

title: ‘Exhausting and demoralizing’: How public media in rural Alaska is responding to federal cuts
summary: KCAW in Sitka is one of more than two dozen public radio stations broadcasting across Alaska. (Photo by Nathaniel Herz/Northern Journal) On July 17, Congress voted to eliminate federal funding for public media across the United States. The cuts, called a “rescission” in Congress-speak, are huge: They will take away some $1 billion that the federal government had previously allocated for the next two years to the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, which provides funding for national outlets like NPR and PBS as well as local radio and television stations with much smaller budgets. That funding is essential to many of Alaska’s local news outlets. It’s no exaggeration to say that losing that money — which was slated to be delivered starting September 30 — threatens the very existence of some stations that provide news coverage and other programming, like emergency alerts, around the state. To get a sense of how local radio and television stations are responding — and what their leaders e...

title: 8.8-magnitude earthquake causes tsunami in Russia and Japan, sets off warnings for Alaska and Hawai’i
summary: Mari Yamaguchi Associated Press TOKYO — One of the world’s strongest earthquakes struck Russia’s Far East early Wednesday, an 8.8-magnitude temblor that caused a tsunami in the northern Pacific region and prompted warnings for Alaska, Hawai’i and other coasts south toward New Zealand. Several locations advised evacuations, but no major injuries have been reported. Native nations along the coast have issued warnings to their communities, such as Quinault Nation and Quileute Tribe in Washington state, Nisga’a Nation in British Columbia, Canada, and Yurok and Wiyot tribes in California. The Canoe Journey in the Pacific Northwest is happening. Nooksack Indian Tribe and Lummi Nat...

title: Bove confirmed by US Senate as federal appeals judge, despite misconduct complaints
summary: Emil Bove, President Donald Trump's nominee to be a judge for the 3rd Circuit, testifies during his Senate Judiciary Committee nomination hearing on June 25, 2025 in Washington, D.C. (Photo by Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images) WASHINGTON — The U.S. Senate on Tuesday night confirmed President Donald Trump’s former criminal defense attorney, Emil Bove, to a lifetime position on the federal appeals bench, in the face of whistleblower allegations and criticism from former judges and advocates.
